subject: Accounting
title: The Human Cost of Enron
description: One-minute skill: The Human Cost of Enron.

hook.text: 20,000 employees lost their jobs. Many lost their entire retirement savings in one week.

buildup.text: Enron encouraged workers to invest their 401(k) retirement funds in Enron stock.

discovery.text: When the stock crashed from $90 to $0.26, employee retirement accounts were wiped out.

twist.text: Executives sold their shares before the collapse. Employees were locked out from selling.

climax.text: CEO Ken Lay died before sentencing. CFO Fastow served six years. Others got longer.

punchline.text: Executives cashed out. Employees paid the price.

quiz.question: What happened to Enron employees' retirement savings?
options: They were wiped out when the stock crashed
They were transferred to other companies
